title: Eugenia ilhensis
intro: Eugenia ilhensis, also known as the Ilha de Itacajá guava, is a species of flowering plant in the myrtle family (Myrtaceae). This plant has been found to possess various health benefits, including antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which have been attributed to its use in traditional medicine to treat digestive issues, wounds, and respiratory problems. Eugenia ilhensis contains bioactive constituents such as flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenes, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with this plant include teas, infusions, and extracts, which are often used to support immune function and overall well-being.
taxonomy: Eugenia ilhensis is classified in the family Myrtaceae. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants commonly known as the myrtle family. Within the Myrtaceae family, Eugenia ilhensis is further classified in the genus Eugenia, which comprises a wide variety of species found in tropical and subtropical regions. The genus Eugenia is a member of the tribe Myrteae and the order Myrtales. This classification provides a framework for understanding the evolutionary relationships and characteristics of Eugenia ilhensis and its close relatives.
